Title: First Hari Raya without husband: Cop's widow focuses on children
Article:
IPOH: For Nur Aini Samiun, Hari Raya Aidilfitri this year is marked by quiet resilience rather than celebration.
The widow of the late Sergeant Mohd Roslan Abd Rahim, who died in a tragic Federal Reserve Unit (FRU) crash last year, said that the festive season felt starkly different without her husband of 15 years.
"I don't feel the excitement of Hari Raya anymore. Whatever he used to do, I continue doing it for the children. Nothing is lacking for them, but for myself, it's not the same. I just bought things for the children. I don't want them to feel sad because I am," she said.
The 45-year-old housewife will be celebrating in Ipoh, her hometown, rather than visiting her late husband's parents in Jengka, Pahang.
She recalled how he had always taken a central role in preparing for festivities, from buying fireworks to decorating their home.
"Now I see other houses decorated, but ours isn't. The lights we had are broken… he was the one who installed them," she said.
Despite his modest salary, her husband took on extra work to support the family during Hari Raya, including helping at orchards and making deliveries.
"This year, I focus on the children. If I feel sad, I don't want them to feel it too," she said.
The absence of their father is keenly felt, especially by the youngest, who once said, "It would be nice if Ayah was here… Mama wouldn't have to do everything alone."
Nur Aini said her husband was a hands-on father, sharing household chores and cooking, including his signature "sup tulang" during Hari Raya Aidiladha.
To keep his memory alive, she shares stories about him daily. "They understand he's gone, but they still wish he was here," she said.
Weekly visits to his grave have become a routine, with extra visits on Mondays.
"When I miss him, I cry as much as I need to. After that, I feel lighter. My children remain my strength," she said.
Even 10 months on, the loss still feels sudden. "The last time we spoke, he said he was on his way back, but then I received the news he was gone. That's what hurts the most," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt considers national committee to tackle illegal e-waste
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The government is studying the establishment of a National Committee to Address Illegal E-Waste to strengthen a coordinated national approach.
The Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry (NRES) said the proposal was discussed at the NRES Special Enforcement Task Force Meeting No. 1/2026, chaired by Deputy Minister Syed Ibrahim Syed Noh on Monday, March 16.
In a statement today, NRES said the meeting emphasised stronger enforcement on environmental issues, including the entry of illegal e-waste.
"Key issues highlighted included the accumulation of e-waste containers at ports, difficulties in identifying owners and illegal factories, constraints in managing seized items, as well as challenges in disposal and legal action," read the statement.
Previously, Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said the proposed committee aimed to address illegal electronic waste comprehensively through a whole-of-nation approach.
The meeting also reviewed enforcement operations such as Ops Bongkar Kontena and Ops Hazard to curb the entry of scheduled waste.
NRES said several strategic measures were identified, including establishing a Department of Environment (DOE) branch at Port Klang, amending the Customs (Prohibition of Imports) Order 2023, and strengthening the management of seized goods and enforcement logistics.
The Special Task Force was scheduled to meet monthly or as needed to ensure coordinated, focused and effective enforcement.
The meeting involved key NRES agencies, including the Department of Environment (DOE), Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan), Forestry Department of Peninsular Malaysia (JPSM), Department of Minerals and Geoscience Malaysia (JMG), and the Department of the Director General of Lands and Mines (JKPTG).
NRES said the government remained committed to strengthening enforcement against environmental offences, including illegal e-waste.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MEF calls for swift government action as Middle East conflict hits businesses
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Businesses are already feeling the pinch from the Middle East conflict with upward cost pressures in logistics, energy and raw materials, Malaysian Employers Federation (MEF) president Datuk Seri Syed Hussain Syed Husman said.
As such, Syed Hussain called on the government to remain prepared and flexible in its policy response should the situation from the Middle East conflict deteriorate.
He said the federation is concerned over emerging cost pressures and operational uncertainties faced by businesses amid the ongoing conflict.
He said the situation highlights the vulnerability of Malaysian businesses, particularly micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs), to external geopolitical shocks.
"While the immediate impact on the domestic economy remains manageable, early signs of rising fuel prices, freight charges, insurance premiums, and input costs are already being felt across key sectors, particularly among MSMEs.
"Although the full extent of the impact has yet to materialise, employers are already experiencing upward cost pressures in logistics, energy and raw materials.
"MSMEs, with their thinner margins and limited buffers, are especially exposed to these external shocks," he said in a statement today.
Syed Hussain warned that prolonged disruptions could trigger a ripple effect, including supply chain delays, higher production costs and reduced competitiveness in both domestic and export markets.
He said while a broad-based stimulus may not be immediately necessary, the government must be ready to act swiftly if conditions worsen.
"Targeted measures such as temporary financial relief, facilitation of access to working capital, and cost-mitigation initiatives would be vital in supporting businesses, particularly MSMEs.
"It is imperative for the government to adopt a proactive and forward-looking approach.
"We cannot afford to take a 'wait-and-see' stance and only begin formulating financial assistance packages after the full impact has materialised," he said.
Syed Hussain also called on the government to develop ready-made financing mechanisms and contingency support frameworks that can be deployed quickly if needed.
He said having pre-prepared solutions would enable swift action, helping businesses weather the crisis more effectively and avoid unnecessary closures or disruptions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang Customs bust smuggling syndicate, seize RM3.9m worth of cigarettes, liquor
Article:
AURIS FAQRURAZZI
BUKIT MERTAJAM: Penang Customs has crippled a smuggling syndicate dealing in contraband cigarettes and liquor worth about RM3.9 million following a series of integrated operations carried out across the district last month.
State Customs director Datuk Rohaizad Ali said the success stemmed from intelligence gathering and public tip-offs under Ops Petasan, which was conducted in stages and involved three separate raids on residential premises and a warehouse identified as storage sites for illicit goods.
"Throughout the operations, we seized millions of cigarettes and thousands of litres of liquor, believed to be intended for the local market," he told a press conference at the Seberang Jaya Customs Complex here today.
Rohaizad said in the first case, a raid was carried out on Feb 9 at a house in Kampung Sungai Lembu about 1.40pm.
"Inspections uncovered various brands of cigarettes and liquor stored in the living room and two bedrooms, with the total seizure estimated at RM97,040.
"A local man in his 40s, believed to be the homeowner, was detained to assist investigations. The case remains under investigation," he added.
In the second case, Rohaizad said a raid on Feb 24 at a premises in Taman Kekwa led to the seizure of contraband worth RM236,572.
He said a local man, in his 50s, who was inside the house during the raid, was also detained to assist in investigations.
Rohaizad said the third case recorded the largest seizure when they raided a warehouse and a lorry in Kampung Manggis on Feb 26.
"Checks found 3.34 million sticks of contraband cigarettes stored in the warehouse and the lorry, with a total value of RM3.64 million.
"Two local men, believed to be the lorry driver and an attendant, both in their 40s, were detained while inside the vehicle during the operation," he said.
All individuals detained have since been released on bail pending further investigations.
Rohaizad said the syndicate used residential premises and a warehouse as storage locations to evade authorities before distributing the goods to the local market.
All cases are being investigated under Section 135(1)(d) of the Customs Act 1967 for possession and storage of non-duty-paid goods.
Upon conviction, offenders may face a fine of up to 20 times the value of the goods, imprisonment of up to five years, or both.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AI business blunders? Some insurers cautiously step up
Article:
AS more businesses trust artificial intelligence "agents" to independently grow their revenues, some insurance firms are stepping in to cover any mistakes – while others are steering clear.
"The whole intent of using advanced AI is to substantially replace human assistance and oversight in decisions," said Phil Dawson, head of AI policy and partnerships at the specialist insurer Armilla.
The trend of "agentic AI" has taken off, with bots handling computer tasks by themselves and businesses trimming ranks of human workers as a result.
"That really challenges some of the fundamental logic of existing insurance coverage," Dawson said.
Companies in the AI race are striving to perfect the technology, but they have not eliminated the possibility of errors such as "hallucinations" in which fabricated output is confidently presented.
AI-related liability risks have been largely accounted for implicitly under insurance policies in what is referred to as "silent coverage", analyst Sonal Madhok and law professor Anat Lior said in a research paper published late last year by the brokerage firm Willis Towers Wa000_96CW39Vtson.
However, they argue that the situation is similar to the liability coverage questions raised in the early years of cybercrime.
"We can expect policies to explicitly address AI in the near future, ending the silent coverage era," Lior and Madhok said.
Insurers are already moving beyond their "wait-and-see approach" when it comes to AI mishaps, according to Jonathan Mitchell, head of the financial sector practice at brokerage firm Founder Shield.
Some standard insurance policies now include "absolute AI exclusion" clauses that expressly deny coverage for AI-related mishaps, Mitchell said.
Dawson cited a commercial real estate firm that tried to have its AI agent covered as a regular employee but had to revert to a special policy.
Founder Shield incorporates "AI malfunction and hallucination" scenarios specifically into professional services policies covering losses the technology causes clients.
The scope of such policies can be extended, for a price, beyond computer networks to cover real world harm such as AI mistakenly ordering too much inventory for a company.
Armilla tests AI models for vulnerabilities before committing to coverage and assesses whether the client's risk management framework adheres to international standards.
But like other insurers, Armilla can decline to take on certain risks.
For example, it avoids providing coverage for anything related to medical diagnostics or applications focused on mental health.
Munich Re, a global titan that does both insurance and reinsurance, provides coverage for companies that design AI models as well as those that use the technology.
"This risk of a model making errors or hallucinating cannot be fully avoided in any technical way," said Munich Re's head of AI insurance, Michael von Gablenz.
"AI systems, at the end of the day, are statistical models; and any statistical model has uncertainty in it," he said.
The AI risk brings with it great opportunity for insurers, though, with von Gablenz estimating the size of the market could eclipse that of cybersecurity insurance.
The Deloitte Centre for Financial Services projects the global AI insurance premium market could grow to as much as US$4.8 billion by 2032.
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rice supply stabilising in Sabah interior districts
Article:
KENINGAU: Rice supply in Sabah's interior has improved following increased stock distribution, State Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industries minister Datuk Jamawi Jaafar said.
He said the monthly demand stands at 470 metric tonnes, with an average daily requirement of about 16 metric tonnes.
He said about 15 per cent, or 70 metric tonnes, of the required supply has been delivered and is being distributed today, exceeding the daily requirement.
Jamawi added that instructions have been issued to expedite the delivery of affordable imported white rice (BPI) in large quantities to Keningau, Sook, Tenom, Kemabong and Sipitang.
"In Tenom alone, a total of 53 tonnes of rice has entered the retail market through Bernas-appointed distributors, namely PPK Tenom (30 tonnes) and Sazarice (23 tonnes).
"Overall, based on the current movement of rice stocks into Tenom, supply is believed to be sufficient, with the cooperation of wholesalers who have delivered stocks to several retail outlets in the area today.
"Findings also show that panic buying has subsided and the situation has stabilised," he said in a statement.
The Melalap assemblyman said rice supply in Sabah is sufficient and advised the public against panic buying following reports of shortages in several interior districts.
He said the state's rice stockpile is currently able to meet demand for up to six months.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor cops monitor 47 hotspots, 38 blackspots during Ops Selamat
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor police will monitor 47 hotspots (traffic congestion-prone areas) and 38 blackspots (accident-prone areas) throughout Ops Selamat 26 Hari Raya Aidilfitri, from tomorrow until Tuesday, March 24.
Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the operation was conducted in line with an expected 20 per cent surge in vehicles entering the state.
"The country's two main border routes, namely the Sultan Iskandar Building (BSI) and the Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB), will also be a focal point due to the expected higher traffic flow during the festive season," he told a press conference in conjunction with the launch of Ops Selamat 26 Aidilfitri here today.
According to him, a total of 2,018 personnel were mobilised, involving 168 officers and 1,118 rank-and-file members from various departments, as well as 69 officers and 663 rank-and-file staff from the Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT).
Ab Rahaman said that Ops Selamat, themed "Kongsi Perjalanan, Kongsi Keselesaan, Keselamatan Diutamakan" (Share the Journey, Share Comfort, Safety First), focused on three main objectives: preventing burglary, minimising road accidents and ensuring smooth traffic flow.
Commenting on last year's statistics, he said Ops Selamat recorded 1,642 accidents, with 26 fatalities, and issued 10,189 summonses for various traffic offences.
"Seven of the fatalities recorded involved motorcyclists," he added.
He advised motorists to plan their journeys in advance and ensure their vehicles were in good condition before travelling during the festive season.
The public was also encouraged to inform the police of their trips back to their hometowns by filling out the 'Balik Kampung' form at the nearest station or via the Volunteer Smartphone Patrol (VSP) application.
As of Monday, March 16, a total of 117 notifications had been received via VSP, and 117 forms had been submitted by members of the public.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran's nuclear doctrine not likely to change, foreign minister says
Article:
DUBAI: Iran's stance against the development of nuclear weapons won't significantly change, Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi told Al Jazeera in remarks relayed by Iranian media on Wednesday.
However,  he also cautioned that the new supreme leader is yet to publicly express his view on the matter.
Former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed early in the US-Israeli war against Iran, opposed the development of weapons of mass destruction in a fatwa, or religious edict, issued in the early 2000s.
Western countries, including the US and Israel, have for years accused Tehran of seeking nuclear weapons, while Iranian authorities have said their nuclear programme is solely for civilian purposes.
Araqchi said fatwas depend on the Islamic jurist issuing them and added he was not yet in a position to judge the jurisprudential or political views of Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new supreme leader.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nearly RM800k from Ramanan's salary, allowances channelled to his constituency
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sungai Buloh member of parliament Datuk Seri R. Ramanan has channelled nearly RM800,000 of his salary and allowances to his constituency since 2023.
His latest contribution involves RM65,200 donated to mosques, suraus, temples, shrines and churches in the area.
This follows an earlier RM80,350 allocation for Ihya Ramadan programmes and grassroots community engagement.
"This is not about the amount, but about responsibility," the PKR vice-president said in a statement.
"What was promised must be translated into consistent action."
To help ease the cost of living for his constituents ahead of Hari Raya, Ramanan, who is human resources minister, also distributed 3,350 food baskets to B40 families and 10,000 servings of bubur lambuk.
Ramanan has served as MP since 2022, after winning the Sungai Buloh seat for Pakatan Harapan in a seven-cornered fight.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rawang family tragedy: Two killed in Kelantan road crash ahead of Hari Raya
Article:
Syaherah Mustafa
TANAH MERAH: A family of six from Rawang, Selangor, travelling to Kelantan to attend a relative's funeral, were struck by tragedy.
Two members, Wan Rokiah Wan Ahmad, 64, and her granddaughter Nur Zara Zakirah Abdul Aliyy, 14, died at the scene after their vehicle collided along the Machang–Tanah Merah road near Bandar Baru Tanah Merah early this morning.
Four others were injured and are receiving treatment at Hospital Tanah Merah.
The victims' nephew, Wan Mohd Azana Wan Mohd Najid, 53, said his aunt had travelled with her husband, her daughter who was driving, and three grandchildren to attend the funeral of her younger sister, Wan Nor Asmahani Wan Mohd Najid, who died of cancer two days ago.
He added that he last spoke to his aunt around 11pm last night while they were near Kuala Lipis, Pahang.
"She told me they had just seen the van carrying my sister's body overtaking their car.
"My sister's body was being brought from Serdang, Selangor, to be buried in Kampung Kutan Hilir, Wakaf Baru, in Tumpat," he said.
"My aunt's daughter, Azwa, called me after the crash in a trembling voice to inform me that they had been in an accident and that her mother was not moving.
"I never expected to lose two family members within less than 24 hours. My aunt and my sister were very close," he said at the Hospital Tanah Merah forensic unit today.
"The bodies of my aunt and her granddaughter will be returned to Rawang for burial at the request of the deceased's children.
"Close family members are waiting for the post-mortem to be completed. This coming Hari Raya will be very sad for our family, but we accept this as God's will," he added.
The fatal crash involved a Perodua Myvi and a BMW 5 Series.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cigarette butts top offences under new anti-littering law
Article:
KUANTAN: Discarding cigarette butts in public places has emerged as the most common offence recorded since the country's new anti-littering law came into effect on Jan 1.
Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Corporation (SWCorp) chief executive officer Khalid Mohamed said about 90 per cent of offences brought to court were related to littering cigarette butts.
"During court proceedings, we noticed that the majority of offences involve throwing away cigarette butts.
"About 90 per cent of cases (relate to cigarette butts).
[[nid:1399697]]
"We urge the public to comply with the law, as the same hands that dispose of rubbish will be responsible for cleaning it up," he said to reporters outside the Kuantan court complex today.
Khalid earlier attended proceedings at the Sessions Court here where three men faced charges for discarding cigarette butts in public places.
They were each fined RM300 and ordered to serve two hours of community service tasks.
He said that to date, SWCorp has issued a total of 1,042 Offence Notification Notices across seven states, involving 820 locals and 222 foreigners for littering in public areas and on roads.
"The statistics reflect proactive monitoring and increased enforcement in high-risk locations nationwide," he said.
Khalid said SWCorp will implement the third series of the community service order for littering offences in six states simultaneously, except Melaka, on March 28, expanding its nationwide impact.
He said the first series, held on Feb 13, involved five states, while the second series on March 12 took place along Jalan Pudu in Kuala Lumpur.
Seven states
— Johor, Melaka, Negri Sembilan, Kedah, Perlis, and Pahang — along with the Federal Territories, have adopted the new anti-littering law.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lorry driver remanded over alleged indecent exposure to underage supermarket cashier
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A lorry driver was remanded for six days after he allegedly exposed himself to an underage girl at a supermarket in Taman Berlian, Yong Peng, near here yesterday.
The remand order against the 32-year-old was issued by magistrate Nurasidah A. Rahman from today until Monday to facilitate police investigations.
The case is being investigated under Section 377D of the Penal Code and Section 15(1)(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017.
The incident occurred at 1.57pm when the victim, who works as a cashier at the supermarket, was approached by the suspect, who had arrived on a motorcycle.
The man initially entered the premises to buy a drink before leaving. He later returned to buy cigarettes, carrying a sling bag.
While making payment at the counter, the suspect allegedly moved his sling bag aside and exposed himself.
The act was captured on the supermarket's closed-circuit television (CCTV) system.
Fearing for her safety, the victim lodged a police report at the Yong Peng police station, leading to the suspect's arrest on the same day.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: City dwellers choose trains to avoid traffic congestion
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: City dwellers were willing to squeeze and stand in crowded trains rather than face daily traffic congestion.
A survey by Harian Metro found that this was the reason most city residents were using the monorail, Light Rail Transit (LRT), Mass Rapid Transit (MRT), KTM Komuter and buses as their main modes of transport to work or carry out daily errands.
Student Hannah Nur Naimah Suhaimi, 21, said she had chosen public transport since her secondary school days due to connectivity factors that made it easier to access various locations.
"I have been using public transport since secondary school until now. For me, it is very easy to reach many places, and with the My50 pass and student card initiatives from the government, it helps me save on my monthly expenses. Usually, I travel earlier because public transport is widely used, and during peak hours, such as going to or returning from work, it is expected to be crowded and require queuing. For me, the situation is still acceptable because the waiting time for the next train is not too long. Even if I drive, I would still face traffic congestion, so it is the same," she said.
The same sentiment was shared by traditional masseuse Jamilah Jantan, 62, who described the country's facilities as among the best and cleaner compared to those overseas.
"I have been using public transport since I was 10 years old during my schooling days in Kampung Baru. Until now, I am still comfortable taking buses and the LRT. If you look at social media, many compare it with other countries such as New York or Jakarta, and most agree that our public transport is cleaner and more organised," she said.
Meanwhile, private sector employee Azureen Zainal, 25, said she had chosen public transport over the past three years to reduce her daily expenses.
"I live in Petaling Jaya and the roads are always congested, so I feel that using public transport helps reduce both time and cost. My satisfaction level when using public transport is about four out of five because there are still many things that can be improved, such as maintenance," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Eastbound traffic from Klang Valley builds up ahead of Aidilfitri
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic from the Klang Valley heading to the east coast began to build up this morning as travellers started returning to their hometowns ahead of Aidilfitri.
A spokesperson for the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said congestion was reported between Gombak and Genting Sempah, as well as from Bukit Tinggi to Lentang.
Traffic flow to the north and south remained smooth, with only minor congestion reported between Bandar Baharu and Jawi due to a broken-down lorry.
"LLM expected traffic volume to increase further from midday following the 50 per cent toll discount, which began at midnight and will run until 11.59 pm tomorrow," he told Bernama when contacted.
Motorists were urged to adhere to speed limits and maintain a safe following distance throughout their journey.
Road users were also encouraged to plan their trips by referring to the Travel Time Advisory (TTA) schedule via the MyPLUS-TTA app, as well as to obtain the latest traffic updates through the toll-free PLUSLine at 1-800-88-0000, the X account @plustrafik, or LLM's hotline at 1-800-88-7752 and its X account @llmtrafik.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Op Selamat 26: Johor police step up enforcement at 85 locations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Johor police have deployed 2,018 officers and personnel for the Hari Raya festive period, with enforcement focused on 85 identified congestion and accident-prone locations statewide.
State police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the manpower mobilisation under Op Selamat 26 aims to meet three key objectives: curbing house break-ins, reducing road accidents, and ensuring smooth traffic flow.
He said the operation would run from tomorrow until March 24, involving 69 officers and 663 personnel from the Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department, as well as 168 officers and 1,118 personnel from other units within the Johor contingent.
"We are also continuing collaboration with agencies such as the Fire and Rescue Department and the Road Transport Department," he said after launching Op Selamat 26 at AEON Bukit Indah here today.
Ab Rahaman said enforcement would be intensified at the two main international entry points, namely the Bangunan Sultan Iskandar (BSI) and Kompleks Sultan Abu Bakar (KSAB), as police expect a 20 per cent increase in traffic volume.
He said 47 congestion hotspots and 38 accident blackspots have been identified for targeted monitoring.
Police will focus on six major traffic offences: speeding, misuse of emergency lanes, overtaking on double lines, queue-cutting, running red lights, and using mobile phones while driving.
"Based on last year's Op Selamat records, motorcyclists remained the main contributors to fatal accidents," he said.
He also urged the public to inform the police of their travel plans through the Vehicle Smartphone Patrol application or balik kampung forms, noting that 234 notifications had been received as of Monday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: The bamboo journey behind Batu Pahat's Lemang Bodong craze
Article:
BATU PAHAT: An entrepreneur here has been travelling hundreds of kilometres in search of the perfect bamboo, the unlikely secret behind the growing popularity of his Lemang Bodong this Hari Raya season.
Lemang Bodong operator Mohammad Amril Azami said that quality bamboo sourced from Pahang and Hulu Langat, Selangor, remained the defining factor in producing the traditional delicacy at its best.
"The bamboo must not be too thick or too thin. The best medium-grade bamboo is found in Pahang," he said when met at Kampung Parit Ju, Jalan Parit Jalil.
To meet festive demand, Mohammad Amril said he typically ordered about 7,500 bamboo sticks to produce 7,000 lemang, allowing for losses from cracks or damage during cooking.
But sourcing the right bamboo is only half the craft. Each stalk is carefully rotated every three minutes over an open fire, a painstaking rhythm that ensures even cooking while preventing the delicate rice from burning.
Workers also wipe away excess coconut milk as it seeps out during roasting, a step he said was crucial to preserving the lemang's soft texture.
"This is why our bamboo does not appear charred. Some even think it is boiled, but it is simply closely monitored," he said.
Each bamboo casing is cut to a precise length of between 17 and 18 inches, a detail that reflects the meticulous approach behind every batch.
This year, Mohammad Amril has prepared 7,000 sticks of lemang, up from 5,000 last year, as demand continues to rise not only during Aidilfitri but also Aidiladha and festive open houses.
What began as a modest venture has grown into a seasonal livelihood, with the entrepreneur employing single mothers and youths to meet the Raya rush.
"Alhamdulillah, the response has been encouraging. It is no longer just about lemang; it is also about creating opportunities for others," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jualan Termurah Madani: 2,000 chickens sold out in just 30 minutes
Article:
Bukit Gantang: About 2,000 chickens, priced at RM7 per kg, were snapped up within just half an hour today at the Jualan Termurah Madani programme held at the Bukit Gantang member of parliament Service Centre in conjunction with Aidilfitri celebrations.
Apart from chickens, eager shoppers also seized the chance to buy beef at as low as RM24 per kilogram, along with cooking oil, eggs, flour, sugar and other essential items in preparation for Aidilfitri.
Roszie Nazra Abdul Razak, 47, from Larut Tin, said she would not miss the opportunity to attend such programmes whenever they were held ahead of the festive season.
"The prices of chicken and meat are much cheaper than at the market or elsewhere, allowing me to save on my Aidilfitri expenses. I bought various essential items, including chicken and meat, and spent under RM500 for everything," she told Bernama.
Azhari Abd Aziz, 63, from Kampung Hujung Matang, said he took the opportunity to buy 40 chickens to distribute to the less fortunate in his village.
"It is always good to hold such programmes, as they really help the less fortunate to purchase their Hari Raya necessities," he said.
Meanwhile, Bukit Gantang member of parliament Datuk Syed Abu Hussin Hafiz Syed Abdul Fasal said the bulk-purchase approach was implemented to provide lower prices to the public without the need for government subsidies.
"We buy in bulk to get discounts, then sell to the public at cheaper prices. It is our way of helping people with the cost of living," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Afghans search for loved ones at Kabul rehab centre bombed by Pakistan
Article:
KABUL: Families and friends of people who were under treatment at a rehab centre in the Afghan capital Kabul searched for their loved ones on Wednesday, two days after it was bombed by Pakistan in the deadliest incident in the months long conflict between the neighbours.
The Afghan Taliban government has said that more than 400 people were killed and 265 wounded in the air strike that took place on Monday night, just as people and staff at the centre were praying, days before the end of the holy month of Ramadan.
Pakistan rejected the claim, saying it had "precisely targeted military installations and militant support infrastructure" - a sharp escalation that has further worsened relations between the Islamic neighbours at a time when the neighbourhood has also become unstable due to the US-Israeli attacks on Iran.
On Wednesday, relatives of people who were at the drug rehabilitation centre gathered at the site looking for their loved ones who were among the hundreds recovering there.
Many said they did not know whether their relatives were alive or dead, or if they had been moved elsewhere.
"We came here looking for our patient, he is missing," said Mazar, 50, who gave only one name.
"We came to find out whether he is well, alive, or what has happened to him."
The relative, Mazar said, had been admitted at the centre for the second time and there was no information about him.
"We checked the lists, but his name was not in the list of the living. Maybe he is injured or has been killed," he said.
Another man who did not want to be named said he had come in search of his relative on Tuesday but was not allowed to enter the centre.
"We did not find his body, nor was he among the wounded, and his name is not on the list of survivors," he said. "We have come again today for more information."
A Reuters witness at the scene saw smoke still smouldering from parts of the compound as firefighters continued to douse small fires, about 36 hours after the bombing.
Pieces of furniture, mattresses and clothing lay scattered among the debris.
The Afghan interior ministry said funerals of some those killed at the centre would take place later on Wednesday.
[[nid:1399638]]
"Some of the bodies were not identifiable and are currently at the forensic department.
Some bodies were intact and were handed over to their families," interior ministry spokesman Abdul Mateen Qanie said.
"Others were completely destroyed, collected almost like pieces of flesh."
Najibullah Farooqi, head of Afghanistan's legal medicine directorate, said bodies were being pulled out of the debris as late as Tuesday night and were being handed over to families.
"Some bodies have been handed over after their identities were confirmed. However, a large number of bodies still remain with us," he said.
Afghanistan and Pakistan have fiercely contested and disputed the target of the air strike.
Afghan authorities said the attack had clearly targeted a well-known rehabilitation centre, a former Nato military base named Camp Phoenix that had been converted into a civilian facility about a decade ago.
Pakistan has said it hit Camp Phoenix, a "military terrorist ammunition and equipment storage site" and that secondary detonations that were visible after the strikes clearly indicated the presence of large ammunition depots there.
The EU, UN agencies and international aid groups have said civilian and medical facilities should not be targeted during a conflict and called for immediate de-escalation.
The conflict between the allies turned foes began last year after Pakistan accused Afghanistan of sheltering and backing militants carrying out attacks across Pakistan, a charge denied by the Afghan Taliban government.
The conflict had ebbed amid efforts by friendly countries including China to mediate, but flared again with Pakistan directly targeting the Afghan Taliban last month and not just locations of Pakistani Taliban militants Islamabad says are across the border.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pahang police focus on 36 black spots, 25 hot spots during Ops Selamat
Article:
KUANTAN: Pahang police have identified 36 accident-prone 'black spots' and 25 'hot spots' statewide for priority attention during Ops Selamat, which runs from today until March 24.
Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the operation, held in conjunction with the Hari Raya Aidilfitri celebrations, aimed to ensure the safety of road users and reduce road accidents, including fatalities, in the state.
"Throughout Ops Selamat, more than 200 traffic officers and personnel were mobilised. In addition, duties such as patrols and security control were intensified," he said after officiating the handing-over ceremony of the Pahang Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department chief at the Pahang Police Contingent Headquarters here today.
At the ceremony, Superintendent Tan Yong Wee was appointed as the new head of the department, replacing Superintendent Azmi Awang Lah, effective today.
Yahaya said the operation also involved cooperation with various agencies, including the Fire and Rescue Department, Civil Defence Force (APM) and Road Transport Department.
He added that more than one million vehicles are expected to use road networks in Pahang, including highways, federal roads and state roads, during the period.
Meanwhile, he advised members of the public leaving their homes to inform the police via the nearest station or through the Volunteer Smartphone Patrol (VSP) application.
"The information provided enabled the police to intensify patrols in residential areas and ensure homes were safeguarded from criminal activities," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Police tracking several suspects in Cikgu Chandra intimidation case
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police are still tracking down several people to assist in investigations into a case of mischief and criminal intimidation at a residential area in Taman Kinrara, Puchong, last week.
This is in relation to a case where three cars were set on fire during an incident in which a 47-year-old man claimed he was threatened.
Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said several suspects are still being pursued to assist in the investigation.
"The seven suspects who were previously detained have been released on police bail," Shazeli said when contacted today.
He said investigations are ongoing before the investigation papers are completed and submitted to the deputy public prosecutor.
It was reported that police have arrested
12 people
, including a woman, to assist in investigations into cases of mischief and criminal intimidation at the home of Chandrasegaran, commonly known as Cikgu Chandra, in Taman Kinrara, Puchong.
The suspects, aged between 30 and 40, were arrested in Banting, Subang, and Serdang, Selangor.
It was previously reported that seven of them, including the mastermind, were found to be directly involved in the incidents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man fined RM1,500 for injuring ex-fiancée
Article:
KOTA BARU: A 20-year-old man was fined RM1,500 by the magistrate's court here for injuring his former fiancée.
Magistrate Rais Imran Hamid imposed the fine on Syed Zamri Syed Zainuddin after he pleaded guilty to intentionally causing injury to Putri Fasha Felisa Mohd Izhan, 19, on Feb 21.
He also ordered Syed Zamri to serve four months' imprisonment in default of payment.
Before passing sentence, Rais Imran reprimanded the accused, saying: "Why are you so quick to raise your hand against someone else's daughter? You are young and have plenty of energy. You should use it to support your family."
According to the charge sheet, Syed Zamri committed the offence at a house in Taman Ehsan here about 5.30pm.
The charge, framed under Section 323 of the Penal Code, provides for a maximum sentence of one year's imprisonment, or a fine of up to RM2,000, or both, upon conviction.
Based on the facts of the case, the incident occurred when the victim was at the accused's rented house, where an argument escalated into a physical altercation, resulting in bruises on her right arm and right forearm, as confirmed in a medical report.
Deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Syamsul Ikhmal Ramli prosecuted, while the accused was represented by National Legal Aid Foundation lawyer Nik Nazriani Nik Ab Rahman.
Earlier, Nik Nazriani appealed for a minimum fine, saying that it was Syed Zamri's first offence and that he had pledged not to repeat it.
However, Muhammad Syamsul Ikhmal urged the court to impose an appropriate sentence as a lesson to the accused and the public.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man held for allegedly filming woman in supermarket toilet
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A man was arrested for allegedly recording a video of a woman in a supermarket toilet in Miri, Sarawak, reports said.
According to a report by the Borneo Post, Miri police said the suspect was arrested at the Permy Technology Park about 2pm on Monday.
Police said the suspect has been placed under remand at the police station lock-up as he is being investigated under Section 509 and Section 377D of the Penal Code for intending to insult the modesty of a woman and gross indecency, respectively.
Police also said they viewed seriously any action that infringes on privacy and threatens public safety, particularly involving women in public spaces.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tetra Pak, Aeon Mall partner for nationwide carton recycling drive
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Global food processing and packaging solutions provider Tetra Pak has launched its Sort & Drop – Recycle the Cartons programme, with plans to roll it out to 27 Aeon malls over the next three years.
The initiative is carried out in collaboration with KPT Recycle Sdn Bhd, with support from the urban sustainability division of the Housing and Local Government Ministry.
Its Asean sustainability director Terrynz Tan said the programme marks its first phase with Aeon as the pioneering mall partner.
She said while the initiative will expand to 27 Aeon malls, the model will also be opened to other malls following the launch.
"We have not set a fixed target at this stage. For example, Aeon involves 27 malls over three years, which averages about nine malls annually. Our expansion will depend on capacity and how effectively we can scale.
"At the same time, we want to gauge the response from other malls. Our priority is to ensure that the rollout with Aeon runs smoothly and is well-executed before expanding further," Terrynz told reporters after the launch.
The programme began with a pilot at Aeon Mall Ipoh Station 18, providing malls with the systems and facilities for food and beverage tenants to recycle cartons easily.
Tetra Pak says if the programme is adopted in all 1,000 malls nationwide, it could divert up to 6,000 tonnes of cartons from landfills each year.
All collected cartons are sent to certified recycling facilities, where paper is turned into recycled paper rolls and polyAluminium parts are made into ECOPEAL roofing sheets and boards, all certified under SIRIM Eco-Label and MyHIJAU standards.
At the launch, Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the programme demonstrates how waste can be turned from a liability into a valuable resource, directly supporting Malaysia's transition to a low-carbon, circular economy.
"Public-private collaboration like this demonstrates that a circular economy can only be realised when all parties work hand in hand - through the right infrastructure, systems, and enabling technologies.
"By strengthening waste management from collection to recycling, initiatives like this drive more efficient and transparent practices that support sustainable cities and thriving communities," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah announces additional public holiday for Hari Raya
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The state government has announced an additional public holiday in conjunction with Hari Raya Aidilfitri.
In a statement, the Chief Minister's Department said the holiday would fall on either March 20 or March 23.
"It will depend on the actual date of Hari Raya Aidilfitri this year.
"This is in line with the Prime Minister's recent announcement," it said.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had announced the same dates as additional Hari Raya holidays
during his visit to Sabah last Sunday.
Under a Special Government Gazette dated March 18, 2026, the Yang di-Pertua Negeri (Head of State) designated the following as additional public holidays — March 20, 2026, if Hari Raya Aidilfitri falls on March 21, 2026, or March 23, 2026, if Hari Raya Aidilfitri falls on March 20, 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jobless man who just completed jail sentence charged over spanner attack
Article:
No pix
KUALA LUMPUR: A 37-year-old unemployed man, who was released from prison recently, pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court today to attacking a masseur with a spanner last week.
Adam Muhammad is accused of voluntarily causing grievous harm to a 30-year-old victim, known only as Sani, with a spanner that is about 40cm long, a weapon which could have caused the victim's death.
The offence allegedly took place at a mall here on March 10 at 8.15pm.
The charge was framed under Section 326 of the Penal Code, which provides for up to 20 years in jail and a fine or whipping upon conviction.
Adam entered his plea before judge Dr Azrol Abdullah.
Deputy public prosecutor Vivien Yeap Jie Xi opposed bail.
"CCTV footage clearly showed the accused bringing a spanner into the victim's workplace, then fleeing the scene.
"The victim suffered severe head injuries and required multiple stitches."
Adam, who was unrepresented, appealed for bail, saying he only recently completed a one-year jail sentence.
He said the incident arose from a personal dispute.
"My wife married that man (the victim) without a divorce first," he said from the dock.
Azrol denied bail and fixed May 14 for case mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump's summit delay casts pall over US-China trade truce
Article:
US President Donald Trump's request to delay the planned summit in Beijing with Chinese leader Xi Jinping casts a shadow over mutual ties that have been stable since their last meeting in October, but is unlikely to cause a major setback, analysts said.
Trump's move shows how the Iran conflict has upended his foreign policy agenda and added war to trade and Taiwan among the spectrum of issues separating the world's two biggest economies.
The delay hits pause on the retooling of bilateral relations just as both sides concluded high-stakes trade talks in Paris on Monday to lay the groundwork for Trump's Beijing trip, originally slated for the end of the month.
The negotiations followed Washington's imposition of new investigations into "unfair trade practices" aimed at rebuilding trade pressure on countries around the world - including China - after the US Supreme Court struck down Trump's global tariffs last month.
"The situation is not in jeopardy, and Beijing still wants to organise the summit, but the US-Iran conflict and the Supreme Court ruling on tariff policies have complicated these efforts," said Zhao Minghao, an international relations expert from Shanghai's elite Fudan University.
"It makes US-China interactions this year more difficult because of Trump's 'war of choice' in Iran."
Zhao added the postponement would mean there was also time for more trade actions.
"The White House says it will continue its tariff policy, but without a doubt, we could see new uncertainties in this regard that impact Beijing's calculations on dealing with the US," he said.
A source familiar with the Paris talks told Reuters before the second day of meetings that China showed openness to potential additional purchases of US agricultural goods including poultry, beef and non-soybean crops.
The two sides also discussed the flow of rare earth minerals largely controlled by China and new approaches to manage trade and investment between the countries.
State-run China Daily described the negotiations in an editorial published on Tuesday as "constructive," but warned Trump that Beijing's "openness should not be mistaken for acquiescence."
"The US side should refrain from taking any further actions that could disrupt or undermine a stable China-US economic relationship. Actions that inject uncertainty — whether tariffs, restrictive measures or unilateral investigations — do just that," read the editorial.
That sentiment would similarly apply to the setting up of the summit, according to Alfred Wu of the Lee Kuan Yew School of Public Policy.
"The fluctuations (in plans) are not ideal from China's perspective, China actually wants something more predictable," he said.
US officials have sent mixed messages about the reason for the delay. On Sunday, Trump told ​Britain's Financial Times he might postpone the meeting if China did not help unblock the Strait of Hormuz.
On Monday, US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Trump may need to delay the trip due to coordinating the war effort - not because of the request that China help police the strait or because of any trade disagreements.
"China's first priority remains keeping the relationship with the United States relatively steady so it can continue focusing on self-strengthening in preparation for the longer-term strategic competition ahead," said Patricia Kim, a foreign policy fellow at the Brookings Institution.
Kim added that China understood keeping Trump satisfied was key to managing bilateral ties, and would still host Trump even though it might be wary of giving him too grand a reception as the US continued military operations against Iran, a strategic partner for Beijing.
"Trump is preoccupied with a war in Iran that has not been resolved as quickly as predicted, so he is keen to ensure that the military and economic fallout from that is contained within the next couple of weeks or so ... which means planning a successful visit to Beijing is next to impossible," said Neil Thomas, a fellow on Chinese politics at the Asia Society.
"From China's perspective, there's been a lot of concern in Beijing about the lack of preparations from the American side for the summit so Chinese policymakers wouldn't mind an extra few weeks to prepare for a more substantive visit."
* The writers are from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Price alignment safeguards supply, curbs leakages
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Aligning fuel prices with actual market costs is critical to ensuring supply stability, as Malaysia does not function in isolation from global markets.
This has been driven by the ongoing conflict in West Asia, which has disrupted global energy supply chains.
The international supply disruptions have, in turn, pushed crude oil prices to surge over US$100 per barrel in the global market.
Petronas Dagangan Bhd managing director and chief executive officer Azrul Osman Rani said the sharp increase has led to a significant rise in import, logistics and insurance costs borne by the industry.
He said maintaining retail prices that are not aligned with market costs could increase fiscal pressure.
"When retail prices are set far below actual market costs, the burden on government subsidies rises significantly and tends to create harmful market responses, such as excessive purchasing and smuggling. This must be firmly addressed," he said on the challenges of price-setting.
The growing subsidy burden could also affect the government's ability to allocate funds to other sectors such as education, healthcare and infrastructure development.
The Finance Ministry announced last week that the retail prices of unsubsidised RON97 and RON95 petrol were raised by 60 sen per litre respectively.
At the same time, diesel prices in Peninsular Malaysia increased by 80 sen per litre.
The new pricing took effect from March 12 until today.
MORE REALISTIC PRICING
Azrul said more realistic pricing in an uncertain global environment helps maintain domestic supply stability.
He noted that accurate market-based pricing helps safeguard the country's fiscal position while preventing market distortions that could adversely affect consumers and local businesses.
He added that these mitigation measures are in line with the cautious approach taken by the government and Petronas, which are actively implementing scenario-based planning to ensure continued supply.
On market sustainability, Azrul acknowledged that Malaysia's subsidised fuel prices, which are significantly lower than the average prices in neighbouring countries, create incentives for leakages and cross-border smuggling.
This occurs when irresponsible parties exploit the situation by purchasing subsidised fuel at much lower prices in Malaysia in large quantities and reselling it abroad for substantial profits.
"When the price gap with neighbouring countries is narrowed, the risks of excessive purchasing, market manipulation and cross-border smuggling by certain parties are also reduced," he said.
He added that price adjustments that better reflect actual market conditions should be considered promptly.
Such efforts would ensure subsidies are channelled to the right target group, curb leakages and wastage, and support domestic supply stability while better protecting Malaysia's economic interests.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: HCK Capital taps Binastra for Subang Sentral revival
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: HCK Capital Group Bhd has appointed Binastra Builders Sdn Bhd as the main contractor to complete the long-stalled Subang Sentral development.
The company said the RM316.5 million contract is expected to accelerate progress, offering relief to more than 2,500 buyers who have been waiting over a decade for their units.
Binastra Builders is a wholly owned subsidiary of Binastra Corp Bhd.
Subang Sentral, formerly known as Remix 1, was stalled due to environmental and structural challenges.
"We are excited to collaborate with Binastra in bringing Subang Sentral back to life. Our focus is on quality and timely delivery for all buyers," HCK executive chairman Tan Sri Clement Hii said in a statement.
"We are committed to completing the project without additional cost to purchasers, while enhancing the surrounding area with infrastructure improvements that benefit the community."
Binastra managing director Datuk Jackson Tan Kak Seng said the company had previously acted as a white knight in rescuing Comintel from its Practice Note 17 status.
"Following this, we were involved in revitalising the Empire Residence project, where three residential developments have since obtained their certificates of completion and compliance.
"With Subang Sentral, we are proud to once again contribute our expertise to revive a long-abandoned development with our new client.
"The project carries not only commercial significance but also strong social responsibility, as more than 2,500 homebuyers have waited many years for their homes," he said.
Subang Sentral is part of HCK's property portfolio, with a total gross development value of about RM15 billion across the Klang Valley.
Total construction cost for the entire project, to be developed in four phases, is estimated at more than RM1 billion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Haj cost stays put: TH maintains RM33,300 fee despite oil surge
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) expects the haj cost for the 1447 Hijrah season to remain unchanged at RM33,300, despite rising global oil prices.
Group managing director and chief executive officer Mustakim Mohamad said the cost stability is supported by existing agreements with airlines, which have already factored in potential fuel price fluctuations.
"We have engagements with two airlines, namely Malaysia Airlines and Saudi Airlines, where the framework agreement already accounts for any increase in oil prices," he said at a press conference today.
Mustakim added that the agreements, which signed several years ago, include pricing mechanisms that help cushion the impact of fuel cost volatility on flight expenses.
"As such, for the 1447 Hijrah (2026) season, we are confident that the haj cost will remain at RM33,300, as the current pricing structure has already taken into account potential increases in aviation costs.
"However, if the conflict in the Middle East continues to last a long time and affects the price of oil, the Haji's cost in the future may be reduced again. It depends on how long the situation lasts," said Mustakim.
Mustakim noted that oil prices have already crossed key thresholds outlined in the agreements, which could trigger cost adjustments.
"There is a clause in the agreement that requires adjustments when oil prices exceed certain levels.
"While some increases are unavoidable, these have already been factored into this year's cost," he added.
Despite this, he said TH remains in active discussions with the airlines to control costs and keep the haj affordable, with preparations underway for the first group of pilgrims departing on April 18.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Top Glove's Q2 net profit up 1.6pct to RM30.76mil on RM1.0bil revenue
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Top Glove Corp Bhd's second-quarter net profit grew 1.6 per cent to RM30.76 million from RM30.28 million a year ago, driven by higher sales orders across most regions.
Revenue for the quarter ended Feb 28, 2026 also rose 14 per cent to RM1 billion from RM883.65 million last year, according to a bourse filing.
The glovemaker said stronger sales orders across most regions, led by a significant surge in Europe, reflected regained market share and sustained customer confidence during the quarter.
It added that higher utilisation of 89 per cent further supported cost efficiency and productivity gains, enhancing competitiveness even as average selling prices softened in line with declining raw material prices.
The company's net profit for the first half of financial year 2026 surged about 94 per cent to RM69.34 million from RM35.76 million, while the revenue rising about 6.8 per cent to RM1.89 billion from RM1.77 billion.
In a separate statement, Top Glove managing director Lim Cheong Guan said that although foreign exchange pressures weighed on performance, the dedicated efforts of its team in ensuring quality, cost efficiency and service excellence helped significantly offset the impact.
"The global environment remains fluid, including ongoing geopolitical developments, and we will continue to manage these external factors prudently and maintain stable operations," he said.
Top Glove said the Middle East conflict has given rise to global crude oil supply disruption, which affects the availability of nitrile latex, a key raw material for nitrile gloves.
The company added that it is closely monitoring these geopolitical developments and working with suppliers to ensure a stable nitrile latex supply and mitigate potential impacts.
In tandem, the group is engaging proactively with customers to keep them updated, while also encouraging the purchase of natural rubber gloves where appropriate.
Top Glove noted that its ability to switch production lines between nitrile and natural rubber gloves offers a distinct competitive advantage in managing raw material supply volatility.
Meanwhile, the group's diversified supplier base, alternative logistics routes and inventory buffers continue to minimise disruption to customer orders.
Executive chairman Tan Sri Dr Lim Wee Chai said that while the ongoing geopolitical situation, including the Middle East conflict, has created significant market, supply chain, and logistics uncertainties, these remain external factors beyond the company's control.
"What we can control and what we will continue to focus on, are our internal strengths: our quality, cost efficiency and service delivery.
"As long as we remain disciplined in these areas, vigilant and united, not only will we be able to manage the challenges ahead, but seize the opportunities they present," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Alleged mastermind in Cikgu Chandra attack out on bail, police hunt for more suspects
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Police have released on bail seven suspects, including the alleged mastermind, linked to the recent attack on activist S. Chandrasegaran, but are still hunting for several others involved in the case.
Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar confirmed today that the seven individuals who were most directly involved have been released, while the search for the remaining suspects continues,
The New Straits Times
reported.
“The seven suspects who were previously detained have been released on police bail,” Shazeli said.
“Several suspects are still being pursued to assist in the investigation.”
The case involves acts of mischief and criminal intimidation at the home of the activist, commonly known as Cikgu Chandra, in Taman Kinrara, Puchong, last week.
During the incident, three cars were set on fire.
Police had previously arrested a total of 12 people, aged between 30 and 40, in connection with the attacks.
Of that number, seven, including the alleged mastermind, were identified as being directly involved.
Shazeli added that investigations are ongoing before the completed investigation papers are submitted to the deputy public prosecutor for further action.

Title: Haj price still RM33,300: Tabung Haji locks in flight deals with Malaysia and Saudi Airlines, future might change if Gulf crisis persists
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Pilgrims’ Fund Board (Tabung Haji–TH) has assured that the cost of performing the haj for the 1447H/2026 season will remain at RM33,300 despite global uncertainties.
TH Group Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer Mustakim Mohamad said that although the ongoing conflict in West Asia could potentially impact costs, TH will absorb any unexpected increases.
He explained that agreements with Malaysia Airlines and Saudi Airlines had already been secured in advance, factoring in possible fluctuations in fuel prices.
“Tabung Haji is confident that the cost of haj will not increase for 1447 Hijrah, as we have already accounted for potential rises in oil prices within the existing agreements.
“Any cost increases have been anticipated, and the haj cost will remain at RM33,300. Tabung Haji will absorb any additional expenses,” he said at a press conference following the TH Profit Distribution announcement today.
However, Mustakim said that TH may review future haj costs if the conflict in West Asia persists and significantly affects global oil prices.
Meanwhile, TH chairman Abdul Rashid Hussain said flight costs had been locked in through a three-year agreement with the two airlines, shielding pilgrims from the immediate impacts of rising fuel prices.
“When we negotiated with the airlines, we secured a three-year agreement that fixed the overall cost. However, there is a clause requiring adjustments if oil prices exceed a certain threshold, which is around US$90 per barrel.
“For now, we are able to absorb the increase and will not pass it on to pilgrims this year,” he said. — Bernama

Title: For a safer Raya: Johor police to focus on 85 routes prone to crashes and jams statewide
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, March 18 — Johor police will monitor 47 hotspots (traffic congestion-prone areas) and 38 blackspots (accident-prone areas) throughout Ops Selamat 26 Hari Raya Aidilfitri, from tomorrow until Tuesday (March 24).
Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the operation is being conducted in line with an expected 20 per cent surge in vehicles entering the state.
“The country’s two main border routes, namely the Sultan Iskandar Building (BSI) and the Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB), will also be a focal point due to the expected higher traffic flow during festive seasons,” he told a press conference in conjunction with the launch of Ops Selamat 26 Aidilfitri here today.
According to him, a total of 2,018 personnel will be mobilised, involving 168 officers and 1,118 rank-and-file members from various departments, as well as 69 officers and 663 rank-and-file staff from the Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT).
Ab Rahaman said that Ops Selamat, themed “Kongsi Perjalanan, Kongsi Keselesaan, Keselamatan Diutamakan” (Share the Journey, Share Comfort, Safety First), focuses on three main objectives: preventing burglary, minimising road accidents and ensuring smooth traffic flow.
Commenting on last year’s statistics, he said Ops Selamat recorded 1,642 accidents, with 26 fatalities, and issued 10,189 summonses for various traffic offences.
“Seven of the fatalities recorded involved motorcyclists,” he added.
As such, he advised motorists to plan their journeys in advance and ensure their vehicles are in good condition before travelling during the festive season.
The public is also encouraged to inform the police of their trips back to their hometowns by filling out the ‘Balik Kampung’ form at the nearest station or via the Volunteer Smartphone Patrol (VSP) application.
As of Monday (March 16), a total of 117 notifications had been received via VSP, and 117 forms had been submitted by members of the public. — Bernama

Title: Man sentenced to eight months’ imprisonment for damaging Sunway City temple
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — A man has been sentenced to eight months in prison after pleading guilty to desecrating a Chinese temple altar in Sunway City, Ipoh, last week.
Perak police chief Datuk Noor Hisam Nordin confirmed the sentencing in a statement today.
He said the man was charged in the Ipoh Magistrates’ Court yesterday under Section 295 of the Penal Code for deliberately defiling a place of worship with intent to insult a religion.
The case began when a local man filed a police report on the morning of March 12 after discovering that a temple altar in front of his shop had been damaged.
“A review of closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage found that an unidentified man had acted to damage the said temple on the previous night,” Noor Hisam said.
Acting on the information, police arrested a local suspect who they believe acted alone in the incident.
Noor Hisam also advised the public not to speculate on the case and urged the community to respect religious sensitivities and the property of others.

Title: No queues, no chaos: Labuan ferry firms report smooth operations this Raya
Article:
LABUAN, March 18 — The usually bustling atmosphere at Labuan’s two main ferry terminals ahead of Hari Raya Aidilfitri is noticeably absent this year, with both facilities reporting smooth operations and no signs of heavy congestion.
Observations at the roll-on roll-off (ro-ro) ferry terminal showed that while ferry load capacity was fully taken up, the situation remained orderly, with no long queues or last-minute rush typically associated with the festive season.
Similarly, operations at the Labuan International Ferry Terminal appeared to be running as usual, with passenger boats maintaining regular schedules and no significant crowd of travellers seen at the terminal.
The calmer-than-expected scenario comes despite earlier expectations of a surge in passenger movement, particularly in conjunction with the extended public holidays for civil servants and the ongoing school break.
Labuan Marine Department director Alimuddin Amiruddin said a total of 26 additional ferry trips have been approved from March 17 to 30 for both directions of the Labuan-Menumbok-Labuan route to facilitate balik kampung travel during the festive period.
He said the additional trips are aimed at ensuring sufficient capacity and smoother passenger flow, even as demand remains manageable at both terminals.
Meanwhile, ferry operator Binabalu Sdn Bhd, with its vessels MV Blue Ocean and MV Galaxy, is providing three trips daily in the days leading up to and after Hari Raya.
Another operator, Labuan Mainland Link, is operating five daily trips using its ferries MV Goodwill Star, MV Putrajaya 1, MV Joystar and MV Kimanis 1 to cater to passenger demand.
Alimuddin said traditionally, the period leading up to Hari Raya Aidilfitri sees a sharp increase in outbound travel from the duty-free island, often resulting in crowded terminals and long waiting times.
“However, this year’s trend indicates a more balanced travel pattern, with passengers possibly opting for earlier departures or alternative travel arrangements, contributing to the steady flow at both terminals,” he said.
He said authorities and operators are nonetheless continuing to monitor the situation closely to ensure smooth operations throughout the festive period. — Bernama

Title: Friday or Monday? Sabah sets extra Raya public holiday based on Syawal date
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, March 18 — The Sabah State Government has announced that an additional public holiday will be observed in conjunction with this year’s Aidilfitri celebration, subject to the official date of the first day of Syawal.
According to a State Government Gazette issued today, Governor Tun Musa Aman has declared Friday (March 20) as an additional public holiday if Aidilfitri falls on Saturday (March 21).
The gazette also stated that if Aidilfitri is instead declared on Friday, the replacement public holiday will be observed on Monday (March 23).
Last Sunday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced a similar arrangement for additional Aidilfitri public holidays nationwide to facilitate travel and planning for the public. — Bernama

Title: DAP leaders publicly rebuke own deputy minister over appearance with activist Arun
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Four DAP leaders today publicly rebuked Deputy Minister M. Kulasegaran for his presence at a court complex with an activist facing charges, saying the action invited “public controversy” and does not represent the party’s official stance.
In a joint statement, DAP central executive committee (CEC) members Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali, Young Syefura Othman, and Syahredzan Johan, along with Bukit Bendera MP Syerleena Abdul Rashid, said they were in “complete disagreement” with their fellow party veteran’s actions.
They said that Kulasegaran should have been more mindful of how his actions would be seen, given his official role as a deputy minister in the Prime Minister's Department.
“His position in the government demands a higher level of sensitivity towards public perception, particularly concerning the principle that justice must not only be done, but must also be seen to be done,” the statement read.
The group also insisted that Kulasegaran’s actions were personal and not a party-sanctioned decision.
“Any act of solidarity towards Arun Doraisamy does not represent the stance of DAP,” they said.
The criticism comes a day after Kulasegaran was seen at the Jawi Magistrate’s Court with activist Arun Dorasamy, who was there to face charges of making statements conducive to public mischief.
In his defence yesterday,
Kulasegaran explained
that he was already in Penang for a meeting and only stopped by the court complex for a brief, coincidental meeting.
He insisted he was not present in the courtroom and had only spoken to Arun to advise him to trust the legal process.
The DAP leaders today concluded by proposing a “middle-ground approach” for the party, urging it to uphold the principle of “innocent until proven guilty” while avoiding any action that could create a perception of interference or partisanship.

Title: ‘Hot spots’ or ‘black spots’, police zero in on Pahang’s 61 riskiest routes for festive travellers
Article:
KUANTAN, March 18 — Pahang police have identified 36 accident-prone ‘black spots’ and 25 ‘hot spots’ statewide to be given priority attention during Ops Selamat, which runs from today until March 24.
Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the operation, held in conjunction with the Hari Raya Aidilfitri celebrations, aimed at ensuring the safety of road users and reducing road accidents, including fatalities, in the state.
“Throughout Ops Selamat, more than 200 traffic officers and personnel will be mobilised. In addition, duties such as patrols and security control will be intensified,” he told reporters after officiating the handing-over ceremony of the Pahang Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department chief at the Pahang Police Contingent Headquarters (IPK) here today.
At the ceremony, Supt Tan Yong Wee was appointed as the new head of the department, replacing Supt Azmi Awang Lah, effective today.
Yahaya said the operation would also involve cooperation with various agencies, including the Fire and Rescue Department, Civil Defence Force (APM) and Road Transport Department (JPJ).
He added that more than one million vehicles are expected to use road networks in Pahang, including highways, federal roads and state roads, during the period.
Meanwhile, he advised members of the public leaving their homes to inform the police via the nearest station or through the Volunteer Smartphone Patrol (VSP) application.
“The information provided will enable the police to intensify patrols in residential areas and ensure homes are safeguarded from criminal activities,” he said. — Bernama

Title: The math is mathing: 2,000 chickens, RM7 per kilo, gone in 30 minutes at Bukit Gantang Raya sale
Article:
BUKIT GANTANG, March 18 — About 2,000 chickens, priced at RM7 per kilogram, were snapped up within just half an hour today at the Jualan Termurah Madani programme held at the Bukit Gantang MP Service Centre in conjunction with Aidilfitri celebrations.
Apart from chickens, eager shoppers also seized the chance to buy beef at as low as RM24 per kilogram, along with cooking oil, eggs, flour, sugar and other essential items in preparation for Aidilfitri.
Roszie Nazra Abdul Razak, 47, from Larut Tin, said she would not miss the opportunity to attend such programmes whenever they are held ahead of the festive season.
“The prices of chicken and meat are much cheaper than at the market or elsewhere, allowing me to save on my Aidilfitri expenses.
“I bought various essential items, including chicken and meat, and spent under RM500 for everything,”  she told Bernama.
Azhari Abd Aziz, 63, from Kampung Hujung Matang, said he took the opportunity to buy 40 chickens to distribute to the less fortunate in his village.
“It is always good to hold such programmes, as they really help the less fortunate to purchase their Hari Raya necessities,” he said.
Meanwhile, Bukit Gantang MP Datuk Syed Abu Hussin Hafiz Syed Abdul Fasal said the bulk-purchase approach was implemented to provide lower prices to the public without the need for government subsidies.
“We buy in bulk to get discounts, then sell to the public at cheaper prices. It’s our way of helping people with the cost of living,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Ports, factories, and illegal junk: Putrajaya gets serious on cleaning up Malaysia’s e-waste mess
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, March 18 — The government is studying the establishment of a National Committee to Address Illegal E-Waste to strengthen a coordinated national approach.
The Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability (NRES) said the proposal was discussed at the NRES Special Enforcement Task Force Meeting No. 1/2026, chaired by Deputy Minister Syed Ibrahim Syed Noh on Monday (March 16).
In a statement today, NRES said the meeting emphasised stronger enforcement on environmental issues, including the entry of illegal e-waste.
“Key issues highlighted included the accumulation of e-waste containers at ports, difficulties in identifying owners and illegal factories, constraints in managing seized items, as well as challenges in disposal and legal action,” read the statement.
Previously, Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said the proposed committee aims to address illegal electronic waste comprehensively through a whole-of-nation approach.
The meeting also reviewed enforcement operations such as Ops Bongkar Kontena and Ops Hazard to curb the entry of scheduled waste.
NRES said several strategic measures were identified, including establishing a Department of Environment (DOE) branch at Port Klang, amending the Customs (Prohibition of Imports) Order 2023, and strengthening the management of seized goods and enforcement logistics.
The Special Task Force will meet monthly or as needed to ensure coordinated, focused and effective enforcement.
The meeting involved key NRES agencies, including the Department of Environment (DOE), Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan), Forestry Department of Peninsular Malaysia (JPSM), Department of Minerals and Geoscience Malaysia (JMG), and the Department of the Director General of Lands and Mines (JKPTG).
NRES said the government remains committed to strengthening enforcement against environmental offences, including illegal e-waste. — Bernama

Title: Nilai urn theft: Suspect demands ransom via WhatsApp for 30 missing remains
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The case of 30 missing urns from a memorial park in Nilai has evolved into an extortion investigation after a suspect contacted the management demanding payment for the return of the cremated remains, police said.
Nilai district police chief Supt Johari Yahya confirmed that the suspect reached out via WhatsApp using a foreign number, claiming to have found the urns.
“The suspect then made a payment demand as a condition to return the remains,” Johari said in a statement yesterday.
“Police are actively tracking the suspect, including identifying the individuals involved and examining the communication network used.”
The urns were first reported missing by the memorial park’s manager on February 26.
Johari noted that the cross-border and digital nature of the crime presented challenges, and police are seeking technical assistance from relevant agencies to aid the investigation.
He said the case is being investigated under multiple sections of the Penal Code, including Section 380 for theft in a building, Section 384 for extortion, and Section 297 for trespassing on a burial site.
Police have urged the public not to speculate on the case to allow for a thorough investigation.

Title: Umbrellas up: MetMalaysia sounds storm warning for KL, Selangor, eight states
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a fresh weather alert for several regions across the country, with heavy rain, thunderstorms, and strong winds expected to persist until 5pm today.
According to the advisory issued at 2.15pm, the affected areas in Peninsular Malaysia include the entire Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur as well as the Selangor districts of Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, and Petaling.
In the northern and central regions, residents in Kedah should prepare for adverse weather in Kulim and Bandar Baharu, while South Seberang Perai in Penang is also under alert.
A significant portion of Perak is expected to be affected, specifically Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang, and Muallim.
Additionally, Cameron Highlands in Pahang and several districts in Johor—including Batu Pahat, Kluang, Pontian, Kulai, and Johor Bahru—are included in the warning zone.
In East Malaysia, the weather warning covers Sri Aman, Kapit (Song and Kapit), and Miri (Telang Usan) in Sarawak. In Sabah, thunderstorms are expected to impact the Interior (Tambunan), West Coast (Ranau), and Tawau regions.
MetMalaysia issues such warnings when there are imminent signs of thunderstorms with a rainfall intensity exceeding 20 mm/hour or when such conditions are expected to last for more than an hour.
These short-term alerts are valid for a maximum of six hours from the time of issuance.
